skill.name: document-generation
skill.description: Generate Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and PDF documents from structured text, HTML, Markdown, and tabular data.
skill.summary: Create and process common office documents with Python-based generators.
skill.author: BOHUYESHAN-APB

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography, reconnaissance, and external-command detections are false positives from prose, comments, and Python code examples. However, the Word and PowerPoint generators fetch user-controlled image URLs and read local image paths without allowlists, and multiple generators build output paths from unsanitized filenames. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, but the skill should not be published until these issues are fixed.

high_findings.title: User-Controlled Image Fetch Enables SSRF
high_findings.description: HTML img src values are accepted from document input and passed to requests.get without an allowlist, private network blocking, or scheme validation. This can let a crafted document force the runtime to contact internal or sensitive endpoints.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source value comes directly from parsed HTML image tags and reaches requests.get. The code shows no allowlist, host validation, or private address filtering.

high_findings.title: Unsanitized Filename Can Escape Output Directory
high_findings.description: Generator methods append an extension but do not normalize or reject path separators in caller-provided filenames. A filename containing traversal segments can write outside the intended output directory.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code combines output directories with caller-controlled filename strings and then writes files. I did not find sanitization that strips absolute paths or traversal segments.

medium_findings.title: Local Image Paths Can Be Embedded in Generated Documents
medium_findings.description: The Word and PowerPoint generators treat an img src that exists on disk as a local picture path. Crafted input can cause accessible local image files to be embedded into generated documents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file existence checks and add_picture calls are direct. Impact is limited by file type handling and filesystem permissions, so this is not rated critical.

medium_findings.title: Remote Image Downloads Lack Size and Type Limits
medium_findings.description: Remote images are downloaded and inserted without content-type checks or response size limits. This can cause resource exhaustion or processing of unexpected data from attacker-controlled URLs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both implementations download response bodies and pass them into document image handling. PPT has a timeout, but neither path limits byte size or verifies media type.

low_findings.title: Output Directory Creation Is Legitimate Filesystem Use
low_findings.description: The static filesystem detections for creating output directories are expected behavior for a document generator. They are low risk when output locations are controlled by the host application.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These lines create output folders and do not execute commands or exfiltrate data. The remaining risk depends on who controls the output_dir argument.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported SKILL.md locations are Markdown prose and a Python import example inside a fenced code block. I did not find shell execution or Ruby backtick execution there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text documents script locations and imports Python classes. It contains backticks as Markdown formatting, not executable shell syntax.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited weak-cryptography locations are prose, comments, parser method names, argument descriptions, or document-generation calls. I did not find DES, MD5, SHA1, or cryptographic use in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows document parsing and presentation generation logic, not cryptographic functions. The matches appear to come from overly broad text patterns.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited reconnaissance locations are CSS styling and comments about HTML parsing or slide placement. I did not find host, user, process, or environment reconnaissance behavior there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are static style text and explanatory comments. No system information collection or command execution is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: requests.get on HTML-Derived Image Sources
dangerous_patterns.description: Image URLs parsed from input HTML are fetched directly by the runtime. Add URL validation, scheme restrictions, private address blocking, timeouts, and response size limits before publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are explicit and use values derived from img src attributes. This is a well-known SSRF pattern in document renderers.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Join with Caller-Supplied Filename
dangerous_patterns.description: Caller-provided filenames are joined to output directories and then written. Restrict filenames to basenames and reject absolute paths or traversal segments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The construction and save operations are visible in the cited files. No evidence of basename normalization or traversal rejection was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Inclusion Through img src
dangerous_patterns.description: A document can reference local filesystem paths in image tags, and the generators will try to embed them. Disable local path loading unless explicitly enabled for trusted inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code branches on os.path.exists and then calls add_picture. The effect is constrained to files the process can read and the library accepts as images.

content.user_title: Create Office Documents from Structured Content
content.value_statement: Teams often need repeatable documents without manual formatting work. This skill converts structured content into Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and PDF files.
actual_capabilities: Generates DOCX files from HTML with headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, and inline text styling.
Creates PPTX presentations from slide-oriented HTML with titles, text, bullets, tables, charts, and images.
Builds XLSX spreadsheets from lists of dictionary-style row data.
Renders PDF files from HTML or wrapped plain text using WeasyPrint when available.
Extracts text from PDF files and converts Markdown to HTML for later document generation.
limitations: Remote and local images need security controls before use with untrusted input.
Generated layouts are basic and may require manual review for polished business documents.
PDF generation depends on WeasyPrint being installed in the runtime environment.
The generators do not validate filenames against path traversal by default.

use_cases.title: Prepare Client Reports
use_cases.description: Convert structured analysis notes into a DOCX or PDF report with headings, tables, and readable formatting.

use_cases.title: Build Presentation Drafts
use_cases.description: Turn slide HTML into a PPTX deck with themed slides, charts, bullets, and supporting images.

use_cases.title: Export Operational Data
use_cases.description: Create Excel workbooks from tabular records for sharing, review, or lightweight reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DOCX report from this outline. Use clear headings, short paragraphs, and a summary table.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Notes to Slides
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these meeting notes into a PowerPoint deck. Use one main idea per slide and include a final next-steps slide.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Data Workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Excel file from these records. Use descriptive column names and keep the rows ready for review.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Document Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DOCX report, a PDF version, and a short PPTX summary from this project brief and data table.

output_examples.input: A project brief with goals, milestones, risks, and a small status table.
output_examples.output: A formatted Word report with section headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, and a table for status details.

output_examples.input: A product update outline with feature notes, metrics, and launch dates.
output_examples.output: A PowerPoint draft with title slides, content slides, bullets, and a chart-ready table slide.

output_examples.input: A list of sales records with customer, region, amount, and status fields.
output_examples.output: An Excel workbook containing one worksheet with clean columns and one row per sales record.

best_practices: Use trusted input or sanitize HTML before generating documents.
Provide simple structure with clear headings, tables, and concise sections.
Review generated files before sending them outside your organization.
anti_patterns: Do not process untrusted image URLs without network allowlists and size limits.
Do not pass filenames that include directories, absolute paths, or traversal segments.
Do not expect publication-ready design from the default generated layouts.

faq.question: What document formats can this skill generate?
faq.answer: It can generate DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and PDF files when the required Python libraries are installed.

faq.question: Can it extract text from existing files?
faq.answer: It includes PDF text extraction and Markdown to HTML conversion for document preparation workflows.

faq.question: Does it support images in Word and PowerPoint files?
faq.answer: Yes, but remote and local image loading should be restricted before processing untrusted content.

faq.question: Is this skill ready for public marketplace publishing?
faq.answer: No. The audit found high-risk input handling issues that should be fixed before publication.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: The runtime needs Python libraries such as python-docx, python-pptx, openpyxl, pandas, BeautifulSoup, pdfplumber, markdown, and WeasyPrint for PDF output.
